summary: Completed static verification of Android Kotlin mic meter implementation with smoothed/peak-hold functionality and dB readout
passed_tests: Mic meter row layout verification - ProgressBar and dB label are inline in horizontal LinearLayout
ProgressBar dual-progress implementation - Uses both progress and secondaryProgress for smoothed/peak hold
dB label formatting - Uses db_format string resource with proper formatting
Peak hold timing constants - PEAK_HOLD_MS (2000L) and PEAK_DECAY_RATE (0.9) properly defined
Peak hold decay logic - Implemented in updateMicLevel() with timestamp tracking
Mic meter reset functionality - All values reset to 0 in stopAudioRecognition()
Noise reduction toggle - Properly implemented with audio effects setup
Smoothing algorithm - METER_SMOOTHING_ALPHA (0.2) applied to raw audio levels
dB calculation - Proper RMS to dB conversion with MIN_DB (-60.0) floor
UI state management - Meter visibility and reset handled correctly
